description: Apply the Capital Asset Pricing Model with its assumptions, plot the Security Market Line, compute expected return from beta, and describe CAPM limitations and extensions

sml.definition: Plot of expected return versus beta for all assets

assumptions.rationality: Investors are risk-averse, utility maximising, price takers
assumptions.homogeneous_expectations: All investors share the same inputs
assumptions.no_frictions: No taxes, transaction costs, or borrowing limits

applications.expected_return: Required rate of return for valuation and capital budgeting
applications.security_selection: Assets above SML undervalued; below SML overvalued

limitations.single_factor: Market beta alone does not capture cross-sectional returns

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use historical market return as unbiased estimate of forward equity premium
anti_patterns.why: Historical premium reflects survivor bias and regime; forward premium is empirically lower and time-varying

anti_patterns.rule: Do not price low-quality, high-beta assets with pure CAPM
anti_patterns.why: CAPM under-prices idiosyncratic risk; empirically low-beta stocks outperform CAPM predictions (the low-volatility anomaly)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat R_f as constant for long-horizon valuation
anti_patterns.why: Term structure matters; use maturity-matched treasury and consider stochastic rate effects
